theory.canonical_statement: \-\ "Buoyant" \ ,\ "System" \-\ "Sun", "Λ\ observed" \-\ 1.5, "Status" \-\ "Buoyant" \ ,\ "System" \-\ "Lab (Riga)", "Λ\ observed" \-\ 0.9, "Status" \-\ "Buoyant" \ \ , StatisticalAnalysis \-\ Mean \== 1.4 ± 0.6,Median , StatisticalAnalysis \-\ Mean \== 1.4 ± 0.6,Median \ \ , (\ Quantitative correspondence \ )QuantitativeMapping := Association , (\ Physical interpretation \ )Interpretation \-\ " Just as object achieves stillness (dz/dt \= 0\) when buoyantlysuspended at ρ\ object \= ρ\ fluid in physical space, Magnetorotational system achieves stillness (d(ω,B)/dt \= 0)when buoyantly suspended at Λ \= 1 in phase space. This explains universal convergence: ALL rotating celestial bodies seek same 'flotation level' in phase space, regardless of mass or size.
